title: Enable production native BLE LCI location writes after authenticated GATT policy
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2.6 implemented and tested the no-hardware /inbound/location parser and HAL bridge, but final security review found native BLE LCI RX currently uses unauthenticated GATT writes, so registering a write resource that mutates HAL location would let a nearby BLE client spoof local-client location. What needs to be done: implement authenticated encrypted native BLE LCI write permissions/pairing policy, add a production Kconfig gate tied to those enforced GATT permissions, register /inbound/location for production native BLE LCI only under that enforced policy, and validate with no-hardware Twister plus physical BLE pairing/write smoke evidence. Evidence: security review identified ble_uart.c RX uses BT_GATT_PERM_WRITE and inbound_coap.c source/interface checks alone are insufficient against BLE-injected IPv6 spoofing; project-LICHEN-zte2.6 therefore keeps /inbound/location registration limited to ztest-only surfaces.
notes: No-hardware loop block 2026-07-02: acceptance requires unavailable physical hardware or real external endpoints (GNSS receivers/time behavior, battery/PMIC ADC telemetry, physical LCI firmware, real BLE app/phone/GATT policy, or board-level validation). No further software-only work is currently separable inside this Bead; resume with physical devices/endpoints available or split a new no-hardware child if a specific simulator/test-harness gap is discovered.
